name: Enumeration/bare-ref recurrence guards (NDEBT-005)

design_notes.what_this_feature_is: Phase-006 F-055: mechanize the two NDEBT-003 defect classes F-027 fixed by hand, as the NDEBT-005 recurrence guard, sourced from the single canonical index NIZAM.json rather than re-derived lists. Two new verify_lib.sh primitives (the 7th and 8th): vlib_enumeration_complete asserts the disk->index (completeness) direction tools/validate.sh C4 does NOT cover (C4 checks index->disk: every listed doc resolves; this checks the reverse: no on-disk governed doc is silently omitted from key_documents); vlib_bare_ref_resolves flags a bare (non-/-qualified) NN_name.md whose basename matches no key_document -- the NDEBT-003b shape C9's /-qualification and C10's three sub-checks both miss. Both are wired into the F-052 self-test surface (fixtures_self_test.sh, a CI job): fixture discrimination (a seeded omission in a scratch dir; static bare_ref pass/fail fixtures) PLUS a real-tree recurrence sweep that is the actual guard.
design_notes.canonical_index_choice: NIZAM.json is the canonical index -- its modules[].key_documents enumerate every governed doc per module. The guards resolve against it (or, in fixtures, against the minimal enum_index.json), never against a hand-maintained list duplicated elsewhere. This is exactly the 'single canonical index rather than re-derived' remediation NDEBT-005 prescribed: guard the canonical source's completeness so any downstream list that SOURCES from it inherits completeness, instead of separately guarding every hand-maintained consumer list (docs/guide/index.html was the NDEBT-003a surface -- and it is deliberately NOT required to be exhaustive, so requiring it would false-positive).
design_notes.false_positive_mitigation: NDEBT-005 was deferred over 'real false-positive risk' (Mode A, contract 027). That risk is real and confirmed: a naive bare-ref sweep over the whole tree flags 19 tokens, ALL false positives -- ecosystem/README.md + NIP-0001 forward-refs to planned-but-unshipped stages (00/04/05/06/08_*.md) and '05_release_train.md' quoted in DEBT.md itself as the historical NDEBT-003b defect. The mitigation, confronted head-on rather than ignored: (1) the enumeration guard uses disk->index, which is unambiguous (a real file is or is not indexed) and green over all four doc modules; (2) the bare-ref real-tree sweep is scoped to methodology/ + standard/ -- the stable, fully-shipped modules where NDEBT-003b actually occurred -- EXCLUDING ecosystem/ (still-evolving, carries intentional forward-refs), registers, and NIPs (quote historical defects). E07 proves the exclusion is necessary (ecosystem/README would rc=1) and deliberate (documented in the self-test). methodology/ + standard/ currently carry ZERO unresolved bare refs, so the sweep is green.
design_notes.dogfooding_note: The feature dogfoods the rules F-053 just codified: E04 and E06 build their scratch probes under a fail-closed $SB guard that refuses an unset/relative $SB and any scratch path inside the repo, with rm -rf -- (methodology/02 Section 11 probe isolation); E03/E04/E05/E06/E09/E10 capture expected-FAIL exit codes explicitly (set +e; ...; rc=$?; set -e; test $rc -eq 1) rather than trusting a pipeline (Section 10(e) / NDEBT-019). The self-test's own enumeration scratch probe uses the mktemp + EXIT-trap pattern the two git-scratch primitives established.
design_notes.next_feature_decision: The next-feature AskUserQuestion aborted (permission stream closed, as before features 050 and 053). Proceeded with the RECOMMENDED ungated root 055 under the phase-006 activation's standing 'Approved. Proceed with the logical next steps.' authorization + the operator's explicit 'Continue from where you left off.', disclosed here and in the PR so the operator may redirect at review (the PR is the steer mechanism; nothing merges without the operator). F-050/F-053 precedent for aborted-question -> proceed-with-disclosure.
design_notes.process_honesty: Single-session collapsed-role execution (046-053 precedent), disclosed-and-permitted by the AGF Orchestrator clause. Design was validated by an implementation spike on the real tree (both guards green; both seeded negatives fail) BEFORE the contract was finalized, so the verification plan reflects verified-working commands. SCOPE: actual ~282 changed lines vs the 160 estimate -- the overrun is the two primitives' heavily-documented python bodies (matching verify_lib.sh's existing header style), the self-test wiring with its real-tree sweep, and the thorough DEBT/CHANGELOG resolution; it is documentation-and-mechanization density, not silent scope creep, and stays well under the phase cumulative gate. No new validate.sh C-check was added (the feature spec places the guards on the F-052 self-test surface, which runs in CI), keeping validate.sh's 13-check count and blast radius unchanged.

files_create.purpose: Minimal canonical-index fixture (modules[].key_documents only) for both new primitives; exercised solely via the self-test, never validate.sh --target.
Positive fixture: every bare NN_name.md resolves against enum_index.json; a /-qualified path and an embedded token are present to show they are not treated as bare refs.
Negative fixture: a bare, stale 05_gamma.md (basename in no key_document) must make vlib_bare_ref_resolves FAIL; a resolving ref shows it flags only the stale token.

files_modify.purpose: Add the 7th and 8th vetted primitives vlib_enumeration_complete + vlib_bare_ref_resolves; header primitive count 6 -> 8 (and the stale internal 'five' comment corrected to eight) with the two new anti-patterns named (NDEBT-005 mechanization).

Wire both guards into the self-test surface: bare-ref static fixture discrimination, enumeration seeded-omission scratch probe (Section 11), and the real-tree recurrence sweep (enumeration over 4 modules; bare-ref over methodology/ + standard/); completeness manifest accounts for the 3 new fixtures (46 total).
[Unreleased] Added: the enumeration/bare-ref recurrence guards entry.
0.24.0: NDEBT-005 Open -> Resolved with the resolution note.

non_goals: No new tools/validate.sh C-check -- the feature spec places the guards on the F-052 self-test surface (itself a CI job), so validate.sh's 13-check sweep and its schema/docs are untouched.

No bare-ref sweep over ecosystem/, docs/planning registers, or docs/nips NIPs -- they legitimately carry forward-refs to planned stages and quoted historical defects; sweeping them would reintroduce exactly the false-positive risk NDEBT-005 was deferred over (E07 proves it).

No change to C4 (index->disk), C9 (/-qualified resolution), or C10 (the three spec-scoped sub-checks) -- the new guards are the missing complementary direction, not a modification of the existing checks.
No change to NIZAM.json content (it is already complete vs disk over all four doc modules) and no new shipped fixtures beyond the three named.

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (HEAD~1 = 5d49fb5, the F-053 merge / PR #32): at HEAD~1 verify_lib had neither new primitive, the enum_index fixture did not exist, and fixtures_self_test had no bare-ref wiring.
verification.command:
set -e
if git show HEAD~1:tools/verify_lib.sh | grep -Fq 'vlib_enumeration_complete'; then echo 'VACUITY: vlib_enumeration_complete already at HEAD~1'; exit 1; fi
if git show HEAD~1:tools/verify_lib.sh | grep -Fq 'vlib_bare_ref_resolves'; then echo 'VACUITY: vlib_bare_ref_resolves already at HEAD~1'; exit 1; fi
if git show HEAD~1:tools/fixtures/enum_index.json >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'VACUITY: enum_index.json already at HEAD~1'; exit 1; fi
if git show HEAD~1:tools/fixtures_self_test.sh | grep -Fq 'vlib_bare_ref_resolves'; then echo 'VACUITY: self-test wiring already at HEAD~1'; exit 1; fi
echo 'E01 PASS: HEAD~1 lacks both primitives, the enum_index fixture, and the self-test wiring (anti-vacuity)'

verification.acceptance_test: vlib_enumeration_complete DEFINED + verify_lib has no sourcing side effect: the primitive is a shell function and sourcing verify_lib.sh emits nothing (its standing contract).
verification.command:
set -e
source tools/verify_lib.sh
declare -F vlib_enumeration_complete >/dev/null || { echo 'vlib_enumeration_complete not defined'; exit 1; }
sourced_out=$(bash -c 'source tools/verify_lib.sh' 2>&1); test -z "$sourced_out"
echo 'E02 PASS: vlib_enumeration_complete is defined; sourcing verify_lib.sh emits nothing (no side effect)'

verification.acceptance_test: vlib_bare_ref_resolves DEFINED + DISCRIMINATES: resolves every bare ref in the pass fixture (rc 0), returns exactly 1 on the stale fixture -- both resolved against the canonical index enum_index.json.
verification.command:
set -e
source tools/verify_lib.sh
declare -F vlib_bare_ref_resolves >/dev/null || { echo 'vlib_bare_ref_resolves not defined'; exit 1; }
vlib_bare_ref_resolves tools/fixtures/bare_ref_pass.md tools/fixtures/enum_index.json
set +e; vlib_bare_ref_resolves tools/fixtures/bare_ref_fail.md tools/fixtures/enum_index.json; rc=$?; set -e
test $rc -eq 1
echo 'E03 PASS: vlib_bare_ref_resolves defined; resolves the pass fixture, returns 1 on the seeded stale fixture'

verification.acceptance_test: vlib_enumeration_complete DISCRIMINATES (seeded omission, Section 11 probe isolation): a scratch dir whose files are all enumerated by enum_index.json passes; adding one on-disk file the index omits returns exactly 1.
verification.command:
set -e
: "${SB:?SB (session scratch root) must be set for probe isolation}"
case "$SB" in /*) ;; *) echo 'E04 ABORT: SB not absolute'; exit 1;; esac
source tools/verify_lib.sh
S="$SB/e04"
case "$S" in "$PWD"/*) echo 'E04 ABORT: scratch path inside repo'; exit 1;; esac
rm -rf -- "$S"; mkdir -p -- "$S/m1"
: > "$S/m1/00_alpha.md"; : > "$S/m1/01_beta.md"
vlib_enumeration_complete tools/fixtures/enum_index.json m1 "$S/m1" '*.md'
: > "$S/m1/02_gamma.md"
set +e; vlib_enumeration_complete tools/fixtures/enum_index.json m1 "$S/m1" '*.md'; rc=$?; set -e
test $rc -eq 1
rm -rf -- "$S"
echo 'E04 PASS: enumeration guard passes a complete dir, returns 1 on a seeded omission (sourced from enum_index.json)'

verification.acceptance_test: ENUMERATION REAL-TREE recurrence guard + load-bearing: NIZAM.json enumerates every on-disk *.md in standard/, methodology/, registry/, ecosystem/ (rc 0 each); a scratch index listing only one methodology doc FAILS against the real methodology/ dir.
verification.command:
set -e
: "${SB:?SB must be set}"; case "$SB" in /*) ;; *) exit 1;; esac
source tools/verify_lib.sh
for m in standard methodology registry ecosystem; do vlib_enumeration_complete NIZAM.json "$m" "$m" '*.md'; done
S="$SB/e05"; case "$S" in "$PWD"/*) exit 1;; esac; rm -rf -- "$S"; mkdir -p -- "$S"
printf '%s\n' '{"modules":[{"path":"methodology","key_documents":["methodology/00_planning.md"]}]}' > "$S/idx.json"
set +e; vlib_enumeration_complete "$S/idx.json" methodology methodology '*.md'; rc=$?; set -e
test $rc -eq 1
rm -rf -- "$S"
echo 'E05 PASS: NIZAM.json complete vs disk over 4 modules; a scratch index dropping methodology docs FAILS (guard load-bearing)'

verification.acceptance_test: BARE-REF REAL-TREE recurrence guard + load-bearing: every methodology/ + standard/ doc resolves its bare refs against NIZAM.json (rc 0); a seeded bare '05_release_train.md' (the exact NDEBT-003b shape) FAILS.
verification.command:
set -e
source tools/verify_lib.sh
for f in methodology/*.md standard/*.md; do vlib_bare_ref_resolves "$f" NIZAM.json; done
: "${SB:?SB must be set}"; case "$SB" in /*) ;; *) exit 1;; esac
S="$SB/e06"; case "$S" in "$PWD"/*) exit 1;; esac; rm -rf -- "$S"; mkdir -p -- "$S"
printf 'A stale bare ref: 05_release_train.md (the file is now 06_release_train.md).\n' > "$S/probe.md"
set +e; vlib_bare_ref_resolves "$S/probe.md" NIZAM.json; rc=$?; set -e
test $rc -eq 1
rm -rf -- "$S"
echo 'E06 PASS: methodology/ + standard/ bare refs all resolve; a seeded bare 05_release_train.md FAILS (load-bearing, NDEBT-003b shape)'

verification.acceptance_test: FALSE-POSITIVE SCOPING IS DELIBERATE + DOCUMENTED: ecosystem/README.md carries legitimate forward-refs to planned stages, so the bare-ref guard WOULD flag it (rc 1) -- which is why the real-tree sweep is scoped to methodology/+standard/ and excludes ecosystem/, documented in the self-test.
verification.command:
set -e
source tools/verify_lib.sh
set +e; vlib_bare_ref_resolves ecosystem/README.md NIZAM.json; rc=$?; set -e
test $rc -eq 1
grep -Fq 'for f in methodology/*.md standard/*.md' tools/fixtures_self_test.sh
grep -Fq 'out of the bare-ref sweep by design' tools/fixtures_self_test.sh
echo 'E07 PASS: ecosystem/README would false-positive (rc=1); the sweep is deliberately scoped to methodology/+standard/, documented in the self-test'

verification.acceptance_test: CANONICAL-INDEX SOURCING (not duplicated lists): both primitives resolve from a JSON index argument (modules[].key_documents via json.load), and the self-test passes the canonical index as an argument (enum_index.json / NIZAM.json).
verification.command:
set -e
awk '/^vlib_enumeration_complete\(\)/,/^}/' tools/verify_lib.sh | grep -Fq 'json.load'
awk '/^vlib_enumeration_complete\(\)/,/^}/' tools/verify_lib.sh | grep -Fq 'key_documents'
awk '/^vlib_bare_ref_resolves\(\)/,/^}/' tools/verify_lib.sh | grep -Fq 'json.load'
awk '/^vlib_bare_ref_resolves\(\)/,/^}/' tools/verify_lib.sh | grep -Fq 'key_documents'
grep -Fq 'vlib_enumeration_complete NIZAM.json' tools/fixtures_self_test.sh
grep -Fq 'vlib_bare_ref_resolves "${f}" NIZAM.json' tools/fixtures_self_test.sh
grep -Fq 'tools/fixtures/enum_index.json' tools/fixtures_self_test.sh
echo 'E08 PASS: both guards resolve from a JSON canonical index (key_documents) passed as an argument -- not a duplicated inline list'

verification.acceptance_test: SELF-TEST 46/46 + WIRED: the two new fixtures + enum_index.json are accounted for, and the five F-055 assertion lines (2 bare-ref, 1 enumeration discrimination, 2 real-tree guards) all report OK.
verification.command:
set -e
out=$(bash tools/fixtures_self_test.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0
printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SELF-TEST OK: 46/46 fixtures accounted for, 0 failed'
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Eq 'OK +primitive +vlib_bare_ref_resolves pass'
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Eq 'OK +primitive +vlib_bare_ref_resolves fail'
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Eq 'OK +primitive +vlib_enumeration_complete '
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Eq 'OK +guard +vlib_enumeration_complete real-tree'
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Eq 'OK +guard +vlib_bare_ref_resolves real-tree'
echo 'E09 PASS: self-test 46/46 with all five F-055 assertion lines present'

verification.acceptance_test: REGRESSION BUNDLE (dogfoods NDEBT-019 rc-capture): self-test 46/46, default 13/13, payload 11/11, e2e green -- each with explicit exit-code capture.
verification.command:
set -e
out=$(bash tools/fixtures_self_test.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0; printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SELF-TEST OK: 46/46 fixtures accounted for, 0 failed'
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0; printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SUMMARY: 13 passed, 0 failed'
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0; printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SUMMARY (payload mode): 11 passed, 0 failed'
bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh >/dev/null 2>&1
echo 'E10 PASS: self-test 46/46; default 13/13 (rc=0), payload 11/11 (rc=0), e2e green'

verification.acceptance_test: DELIVERABLE CONFINEMENT: HEAD~1..HEAD touches exactly the seven contracted files and no .agent path.
verification.command:
set -e
test "$(git diff --name-only HEAD~1..HEAD | LC_ALL=C sort | tr '\n' ' ')" = 'CHANGELOG.md docs/planning/DEBT.md tools/fixtures/bare_ref_fail.md tools/fixtures/bare_ref_pass.md tools/fixtures/enum_index.json tools/fixtures_self_test.sh tools/verify_lib.sh '
test -z "$(git diff --name-only HEAD~1..HEAD -- .agent/)"
echo 'E11 PASS: confined to the 7 contracted files, zero .agent'

verification.acceptance_test: DOCS/DEBT SYNCED: DEBT 0.24.0 has NDEBT-005 in Resolved (not Open); verify_lib header documents eight primitives; CHANGELOG [Unreleased] names the F-055 entry.
verification.command:
set -e
grep -Fq 'version: 0.24.0' docs/planning/DEBT.md
python3 - <<'PY'
import re
sect=None; resolved=set(); openset=set()
for l in open('docs/planning/DEBT.md'):
    if l.startswith('## Open'): sect='open'
    elif l.startswith('## Resolved'): sect='resolved'
    m=re.match(r'\| (NDEBT-\d+) ', l)
    if m and sect=='resolved': resolved.add(m.group(1))
    if m and sect=='open': openset.add(m.group(1))
assert 'NDEBT-005' in resolved, 'NDEBT-005 not Resolved'
assert 'NDEBT-005' not in openset, 'NDEBT-005 still Open'
print('debt ok')
PY
grep -Fq 'exactly the eight documented primitives' tools/verify_lib.sh
sed -n '/## \[Unreleased\]/,/## \[0.7.0\]/p' CHANGELOG.md | grep -Fq 'Enumeration/bare-ref recurrence guards'
echo 'E12 PASS: DEBT 0.24.0 (NDEBT-005 Resolved, not Open); verify_lib header eight; CHANGELOG names the F-055 entry'

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured output is in .agent/evidence/055-verify-NN.txt (12 entries), plus the consolidated .agent/evidence/055/verification.txt (12 EXIT:0 markers) and .agent/evidence/055/diff.patch. Commands run from the repo root at deliverable commit f2c109a (HEAD), with HEAD~1 = the F-053 merge 5d49fb5 as the anti-vacuity anchor; the scratch probes (E04/E05/E06) build under a fail-closed session-scratch root $SB and never mutate the real tree (Section 11 probe isolation).

approvals.process_note: Operator-directed phase execution (ungated DAG root). The feature-choice AskUserQuestion aborted; proceeded with the recommended 055 under standing authorization + the operator's 'Continue', disclosed here + in the PR for redirect-at-review (F-050/F-053 precedent). No separate Loop-1 gate agents; the 12-entry suite executed live with captured evidence (dogfooding the NDEBT-019 rc-capture + Section 11 probe-isolation rules F-053 added); QA record .agent/qa/055.json documents the independent re-run. Scope OVER the 160 estimate at ~282 changed lines (documented in design_notes.process_honesty), under the phase cumulative gate.
